title: Fixing Duplicate Key Insertion in TRIE

statement.markdown: Given a string, construct a TRIE (prefix tree) and implement a method to insert a word into the Trie. However, there's a logical bug in the code where it fails to handle duplicate words correctly. You need to fix the code to handle duplicate words correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string of words separated by spaces, and each word will be a sequence of alphabets.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the TRIE structure after inserting all the words.
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), where n is the total number of words. Space complexity: O(n), where n is the total number of unique words.

examples.explanation: The given words are inserted into the Trie. Note that 'banana' is a duplicate word.

hints.text: The bug is related to how duplicate words are handled in the Trie.

editorial.title: Fixing Duplicate Key Insertion in TRIE
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use a flag to track whether a word is being inserted for the first time or not.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to search for the word in the Trie before inserting it, and if it exists, do nothing.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a flag to track whether a word is being inserted for the first time or not.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution correctly handles duplicate words by using a flag to track the insertion status.
editorial.dryRun: Insert 'apple', 'banana' and check if 'banana' is a duplicate. Then insert 'cat'.
